reports.raw_transcript: When Mary Lennox was sent to Misselthwaite Manor to live with her uncle, everybody said she was the most disagreeable-looking child ever seen. It was true, too. She had a little thin face and a little thin body, thin light hair and a sour expression. Her hair was yellow, and her face was yellow because she had been born in India and had always been ill in one way or another. Her father had held a position under the English government and had always been busy and ill himself, And her mother had been a great beauty who cared only to go to parties and amuse herself with gay people. She had not wanted a little girl at all. And when Mary was born, she handed her over to the care of an Ayah, who was made to understand that if she wished to please the Mem Sahib, she must keep the child out of sight as much as possible.
reports.evaluated_transcript: When Mary Lennox was sent to Misselthwaite Manor to live with her uncle, everybody said she was the most disagreeable-looking child ever seen. It was true, too. She had a little thin face and a little thin body, thin light hair and a sour expression. Her hair was yellow, and her face was yellow because she had been born in India and had always been ill in one way or another. Her father had held a position under the English government and had always been busy and ill himself, And her mother had been a great beauty who cared only to go to parties and amuse herself with gay people. She had not wanted a little girl at all. And when Mary was born, she handed her over to the care of an Ayah, who was made to understand that if she wished to please the Mem Sahib, she must keep the child out of sight as much as possible.

reports.raw_transcript: "'Was going to dress me,' demanded Mary. Martha sat up on her heels again and stared. She spoke in broad Yorkshire in her amazement. "'Canna tha dress thysen,' she said. "'What do you mean, I don't understand your language?' said Mary. "'Eh, I forgot,' Martha said. "'Mrs. Medlock told me I'd have to be careful or you wouldn't know what I was sayin. I mean, can't you put on your own clothes?' "'No,' answered Mary, quite indignantly. "'I never did in my life. My Ayah dressed me, of course.' "'Well,' said Martha, evidently not in the least aware that she was impudent. It's time Tha should learn. Tha cannot begin younger. It'll do thee good to wait on thy Senna bit. My mother always said she couldn't see why grand people's children didn't turn out fair fools, what with nurses and bein', washed and dressedin', took out to walk as if they was puppies.
reports.evaluated_transcript: "'Was going to dress me,' demanded Mary. Martha sat up on her heels again and stared. She spoke in broad Yorkshire in her amazement. "'Canna tha dress thysen,' she said. "'What do you mean, I don't understand your language?' said Mary. "'Eh, I forgot,' Martha said. "'Mrs. Medlock told me I'd have to be careful or you wouldn't know what I was sayin. I mean, can't you put on your own clothes?' "'No,' answered Mary, quite indignantly. "'I never did in my life. My Ayah dressed me, of course.' "'Well,' said Martha, evidently not in the least aware that she was impudent. It's time Tha should learn. Tha cannot begin younger. It'll do thee good to wait on thy Senna bit. My mother always said she couldn't see why grand people's children didn't turn out fair fools, what with nurses and bein', washed and dressedin', took out to walk as if they was puppies.

reports.raw_transcript: "'If you scream another scream,' she said, "'I'll scream, too, and I can scream louder than you can and I'll frighten you, I'll frighten you.'" He actually had stopped screaming because she had startled him so. The scream which had been coming almost choked him. The tears were streaming down his face and he shook all over. "'I can't stop,' he gasped and sobbed. "'I can't, I can't!' "'You can!' shouted Mary. "'Half that ails you is hysterics and temper, just hysterics, hysterics, hysterics.' And she stamped each time she said it. I felt the lump, I felt it, choked out Colin. I knew I should, I shall have a hunch on my back and then I shall die. And he began to writhe again and turned on his face and sobbed and wailed but he didn't scream. You didn't feel a lump, contradicted Mary fiercely. If you did it was only a hysterical lump. Hysterics makes lumps. There's nothing the matter with your horrid back, nothing but hysterics. Turn over and let me look at it.
reports.evaluated_transcript: "'If you scream another scream,' she said, "'I'll scream, too, and I can scream louder than you can and I'll frighten you, I'll frighten you.'" He actually had stopped screaming because she had startled him so. The scream which had been coming almost choked him. The tears were streaming down his face and he shook all over. "'I can't stop,' he gasped and sobbed. "'I can't, I can't!' "'You can!' shouted Mary. "'Half that ails you is hysterics and temper, just hysterics, hysterics, hysterics.' And she stamped each time she said it. I felt the lump, I felt it, choked out Colin. I knew I should, I shall have a hunch on my back and then I shall die. And he began to writhe again and turned on his face and sobbed and wailed but he didn't scream. You didn't feel a lump, contradicted Mary fiercely. If you did it was only a hysterical lump. Hysterics makes lumps. There's nothing the matter with your horrid back, nothing but hysterics. Turn over and let me look at it.

reports.raw_transcript: When Mrs. Medlock looked, she threw up her hands and gave a little shriek, and every man and woman servant within hearing bolted across the servants' hall and stood looking through the window with their eyes almost starting out of their heads. Across the lawn came the Master of Mizzlethwaite, and he looked as many of them had never seen him. And by his side with his head up in the air, and his eyes full of laughter walked as strongly and steadily as any boy in Yorkshire, Master Colin.
reports.evaluated_transcript: When Mrs. Medlock looked, she threw up her hands and gave a little shriek, and every man and woman servant within hearing bolted across the servants' hall and stood looking through the window with their eyes almost starting out of their heads. Across the lawn came the Master of Mizzlethwaite, and he looked as many of them had never seen him. And by his side with his head up in the air, and his eyes full of laughter walked as strongly and steadily as any boy in Yorkshire, Master Colin.

reports.transcript: Was going to dress me, demanded Mary. Martha sat up on her heels again and stared. She spoke in broad Yorkshire in her amazement. Can a tha dress thy sen, she said. What do you mean? I don't understand your language, said Mary. Ay, I forgot, Martha said. Mrs. Medlock told me I'd have to be careful or you wouldn't know what I was sayin. opening_india.raw_transcript: When Mary Lennox was sent to Mizzlethwaite Manor to live with her uncle, everybody said she was the most disagreeable looking child ever seen. It was true too. She had a little thin face and a little thin body, thin light hair and a sour expression. Her hair was yellow and her face was yellow because she had been born in India and had always been ill in one way or another. Her father had held a position under the English government and had always been busy and ill himself. And her mother had been a great beauty who cared only to go to parties and amuse herself with gay people. She had not wanted a little girl at all. And when Mary was born, she handed her over to the care of an Ayah, who was made to understand that if she wished to please the Mem Sahib, she must keep the child out of sight as much as possible.

yorkshire_dialogue.raw_transcript: "'Was going to dress me,' demanded Mary. Martha sat up on her heels again and stared. She spoke in broad Yorkshire in her amazement. "'Canna thou dress thy Sen?' she said. "'What do you mean, I don't understand your language?' said Mary. "'Eh, I forgot,' Martha said. "'Mrs. Medlock told me I'd have to be careful or you wouldn't know what I was saying. I mean, can't you put on your own clothes?' "'No,' answered Mary, quite indignantly. "'I never did in my life. My eye addressed me, of course.' Well, said Martha, evidently not in the least aware that she was impudent, it's time Tha should learn. Tha cannot begin younger. It'll do thee good to wait on thy son a bit. My mother always said she couldn't see why grand people's children didn't turn out fair fools. What with nurses and being washed and dressed and took out to walk as if they was puppies.

mary_colin_emotion.raw_transcript: "'If you scream another scream,' she said, "'I'll scream, too, and I can scream louder than you can and I'll frighten you, I'll frighten you.'" He actually had stopped screaming because she had startled him so. The scream which had been coming almost choked him. The tears were streaming down his face and he shook all over. "'I can't stop,' he gasped and sobbed. "'I can't, I can't!' "'You can!' shouted Mary. "'Half that ails you is hysterics and temper, just hysterics, hysterics, hysterics.' And she stamped each time she said it. I felt the lump, I felt it, choked out Colin. I knew I should, I shall have a hunch on my back and then I shall die. And he began to writhe again and turned on his face and sobbed and wailed but he didn't scream. You didn't feel a lump, contradicted Mary fiercely. If you did it was only a hysterical lump. Hysterics makes lumps. There's nothing the matter with your horrid back, nothing but hysterics. Turn over and let me look at it.

Across the lawn came the master of Mizzlethwaite, and he looked as many of them had never seen him. And by his side with his head up in the air, and his eyes full of laughter walked as strongly and steadily as any boy in Yorkshire, Master Colin.

ending_return.raw_transcript: When Mrs. Medlock looked, she threw up her hands and gave a little shriek, and every man and woman servant within hearing bolted across the servants' hall and stood looking through the window with their eyes almost starting out of their heads. Across the lawn came the Master of Misselthwaite, and he looked as many of them had never seen him. And by his side with his head up in the air, and his eyes full of laughter walked as strongly and steadily as any boy in Yorkshire, Master Colin. Thank you for joining us today, and have a great weekend.
